NCT01179048.name: LEADER
NCT01179048.group: Liraglutide 1.8 mg SC daily | GLP-1 RA (T2D + high CV risk; vs placebo)

allOutcomes.title: 3-point MACE liraglutide vs placebo (LEADER primary; median 3.8 yr)

NCT01179048.snippet: Marso SP et al. NEJM 2016;375:311-322 (LEADER). First GLP-1 RA CV-benefit signal; 3-point MACE primary endpoint.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NCT01720446.name: SUSTAIN-6
NCT01720446.group: Semaglutide 0.5/1 mg SC weekly | GLP-1 RA (T2D + high CV risk; vs placebo)

allOutcomes.title: 3-point MACE semaglutide pooled vs placebo (SUSTAIN-6 primary; median 2.1 yr)

NCT01720446.snippet: Marso SP et al. NEJM 2016;375:1834-1844 (SUSTAIN-6). Pre-approval CVOT; non-inferiority with superiority demonstrated.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NCT01394952.name: REWIND
NCT01394952.group: Dulaglutide 1.5 mg SC weekly | GLP-1 RA (T2D +/- established CVD; vs placebo)

allOutcomes.title: 3-point MACE dulaglutide vs placebo (REWIND primary; median 5.4 yr)

NCT01394952.snippet: Gerstein HC et al. Lancet 2019;394:121-130 (REWIND). Broad population including primary prevention; longest median follow-up in class.

NCT02692716.name: PIONEER-6
NCT02692716.group: Oral semaglutide 14 mg daily | GLP-1 RA (T2D + high CV risk; vs placebo)

allOutcomes.title: 3-point MACE oral semaglutide vs placebo (PIONEER-6 primary non-inferiority; median 15.9 mo)

NCT02692716.snippet: Husain M et al. NEJM 2019;381:841-851 (PIONEER-6). Pre-approval non-inferiority CVOT for oral semaglutide.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 50 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NCT01147250.name: ELIXA
NCT01147250.group: Lixisenatide 20 mcg SC daily | GLP-1 RA (T2D + recent ACS; vs placebo)

allOutcomes.title: 4-point MACE lixisenatide vs placebo (ELIXA primary; includes unstable-angina hosp; median 2.1 yr)

NCT01147250.snippet: Pfeffer MA et al. NEJM 2015;373:2247-2257 (ELIXA). Post-ACS T2D; non-inferiority met, no CV benefit signal. Uses 4-point MACE (declared).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 30 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Men and women who experienced a spontaneous ACS event (i.e., ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction (STEMI) or non-ST-segment elevation MI (NSTEMI) or unstable angina) with a documented elevation above the normal reference range of a cardiac biomarker (Troponin or Creatinine Kinase (CK)-MB) and the clinical presentation consistent with an ACS which lead to admission to an acute care facility, within 180 days following the ACS event and prior to screening.~* Participant...

evidence.label: Demographics (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Race: Caucasian/White 2318, Caucasian/White 2258, Caucasian/White 4576, Black 103, Black 118. NOTE: figures are total-arms aggregate from CT.gov baseline-measurements module; per-arm breakdown available in AACT raw data. RACE caveat: review trial-population racial composition for external-validity considerations.

NMA_CONFIG.outcome_label: Trial-primary MACE composite (3-point or 4-point CV death + non-fatal MI + non-fatal stroke +/- unstable-angina hospitalisation)
NMA_CONFIG.note: k=8 Pbo-controlled CVOTs; 8 GLP-1 RA nodes connected to Placebo center. Star network → no closed-loop consistency testing. Each drug anchored by single Pbo-controlled CVOT (no within-drug replication from a second CVOT). Indirect inter-drug contrasts via Pbo anchor.

title: RapidMeta Cardiology | GLP-1 RA Class NMA for MACE in T2D CV-Outcomes v1.0
